---
title: "Cellerator Pipeline"
---

Cellerator is a GPU-oriented ML compute stack for large sparse single-cell datasets. It sits on top of `CellShard`, which owns data handling: canonical sparse storage, optional ingest, CSPACK delivery, compaction/finalization, execution staging, and generic sparse row/feature masking for runtime layouts. Cellerator owns model-facing math over CellShard matrices, including reusable sparse compute, trajectory logic, model workflows, quantized kernels, and Torch interop boundaries. Biology-facing preprocessing policy and workflow APIs live in `CellShardPreprocess`.

This documentation is not a paper workspace. It is the active source for explaining how the Cellerator pipeline is organized, what each stage owns, and where local operations still live outside the packaged product surface.

The canonical scope boundary lives in `../scope.md`; current temporary or out-of-scope surfaces are tracked in `../out_of_scope_inventory.md`.

## What This Documentation Covers

- The ownership split from optional CellShard ingest to runtime execution.
- The contract between Cellerator and `CellShard`.
- Where CellShardPreprocess, compute, trajectory, model, quantized, and Torch-facing surfaces fit.
- The current quantized transfer-versus-decode architecture questions for sparse inference.
- The current local-only download and preprocessing helpers under `docs/pipeline/`.
- The build and test entrypoints that validate the pipeline.

## Pipeline At A Glance

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    A[Source Matrices and Metadata] --> B[CellShard Optional Ingest]
    B --> C[Canonical Emission]
    C --> D[CellShard csh5 and Pack Generations]
    D --> E[Cellerator Matrix Math]
    E --> I[CellShardPreprocess Policy API]
    I --> F[Trajectory and Model Workflows]
    D --> G[CellShardPreprocess Workbench]
    D --> H[Torch Export Boundary]
```

## Core Rules

- CellShard owns data handling: persisted sparse storage, optional source ingest, layout publication, compaction/finalization, execution-CSPACK delivery, and owner-side runtime coordination.
- `src/compute/` is the authoritative home for reusable Cellerator math over CellShard matrices.
- CellShardPreprocess owns biology-facing preprocessing policy, state validation, workflow APIs, and the native workbench.
- `src/models/`, `src/trajectory/`, `src/quantized/`, and `src/torch/` sit on top of those lower-level surfaces instead of redefining them.
- `docs/pipeline/` remains local-only operational glue. It can call external tools or built binaries, but it is not part of the CMake or install surface.
- Scope drift should be recorded in `../out_of_scope_inventory.md` before it is treated as Cellerator-owned.

## Read Next

- [Architecture](architecture.qmd) for the high-level ownership split.
- [Ingest And Runtime](ingest_and_runtime.qmd) for the bounded-memory ingest and owner-service contract.
- [Compute And Models](compute_and_models.qmd) for how downstream pipeline stages fit together.
- [Quantized Transfer Architecture](quantized_transfer_architecture.qmd) for the current Volta-oriented quantized sparse execution report.
- [Local Pipeline Ops](local_pipeline_ops.qmd) for the shell wrappers and manifest-driven local workflows under `docs/pipeline/`.
